preview_text:
The model favors Brazil at 76%, with Haiti offered only a 4% chance of victory. The gap between the two sides is substantial, reflecting a clear hierarchical difference in expected performance. Haiti's win probability represents a minor tail outcome in this matchup.

The 20% draw probability signals a plausible alternative outcome—Brazil is favored but not overwhelmingly so in any single scenario. The win-draw-loss spread shows moderate separation rather than a blowout forecast, suggesting competitive play is possible even if Brazil is the probable winner.

The market consensus deviates meaningfully from the model. Sportsbooks assign Brazil 89% and Haiti 3%, an 13 percentage point gap above the model's 76%. Polymarket pricing is even more extreme, pushing Brazil to 91%. The dashboard does not claim to know which source is correct; the divergence likely reflects different underlying assumptions about team strength or match conditions that the numerical inputs alone do not reveal.
